visa_free.notes: Algerian citizens enter Syria without a visa. Listed as visa-free in Wikipedia's Visa policy of Syria article (citing Syrian government policy). Max stay not confirmed on official Syrian government domains.

Jordanian citizens enter Syria without a visa and without paying visa fees. Listed as fee-exempt in multiple sources referencing the transitional government's July 2025 visa fee decree (Decree No. 148/8). Max stay not confirmed on official Syrian government domains.

Lebanese citizens with ordinary passports who hold a valid residence permit in another country OR have at least one Syrian family member (parent, spouse, sibling) can enter visa-free. Lebanese nationals without foreign residency permits and without Syrian family connections require prior visa authorization. The syrianguides.com source (referencing official fee decrees) lists Lebanon with a fee of $60 (distinct from other visa-free nationals), suggesting Lebanon's status is more nuanced. Source noted above (inyourpocket.com, Wikipedia) list Lebanon as visa-free with the family/residence caveat. Max stay not confirmed on official Syrian government domains.

Mauritanian citizens enter Syria without a visa and without visa fees per multiple sources referencing the transitional government's July 2025 fee decree. Max stay not confirmed on official Syrian government domains.

Moroccan citizens enter Syria without a visa. Listed as visa-free in Wikipedia's Visa policy of Syria article (citing Syrian government policy). Max stay not confirmed on official Syrian government domains.

Sudanese citizens enter Syria without a visa. Listed as visa-free in Wikipedia's Visa policy of Syria article (citing Syrian government policy). Max stay not confirmed on official Syrian government domains.

Tunisian citizens enter Syria without a visa. Listed as visa-free in Wikipedia's Visa policy of Syria article (citing Syrian government policy). Max stay not confirmed on official Syrian government domains.

Turkish citizens enter Syria without a visa. Listed as visa-free in Wikipedia's Visa policy of Syria article (citing Syrian government policy). Max stay not confirmed on official Syrian government domains.

Yemeni citizens enter Syria without a visa. Listed as visa-free in Wikipedia's Visa policy of Syria article (citing Syrian government policy). Max stay not confirmed on official Syrian government domains.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Serbian citizens are listed as fee-exempt (zero-fee visa on arrival) under multiple sources referencing the transitional government's July 2025 fee decree (Decree No. 148/8). However, the Serbian MFA states that Serbian citizens (including diplomatic and official passports) require a visa for Syria, suggesting that even the fee-exempt status may require a VOA stamp rather than true visa-free admission. Max stay not confirmed on official sources. Fee: $0.

Malaysian citizens obtain a visa on arrival. Wikipedia's Visa policy of Syria article lists Malaysia as receiving a 15-day visa on arrival. Secondary sources (syrianguides.com) list Malaysia as fee-exempt under Decree No. 148/8. Fee: $0.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, UAE)
visa_on_arrival.notes: GCC member state nationals receive a visa on arrival. Wikipedia's Visa policy of Syria article lists GCC nationals as eligible for VOA. Duration not specified. Fees not confirmed from official sources.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: All other nationalities except Iran and Israel
visa_on_arrival.notes: From January 2025, the Syrian transitional government allows essentially all nationalities (except Iran and Israel) to obtain a visa on arrival at Damascus and Aleppo airports and at land borders with Lebanon and Jordan. Fees vary by nationality (reciprocity principle), effective July 2025 per Decree No. 148/8. Visa is issued as a stamp on a separate sheet rather than in passport. Air travelers to Damascus or Aleppo airports require prior entry approval (OK to board) obtained 48 hours before departure for most nationalities. Notable fee examples (secondary sources, not confirmed on official Syrian government domains): EU/EFTA/Schengen countries $75; UK $100; Canada $100; USA $150-200; Australia/New Zealand $150; Russia $50 (15-day); Brazil $50. Israeli nationals and those with Israeli passport stamps are denied entry. Iranian nationals require an advance visa from a Syrian embassy and are not eligible for VOA.

e_visa.notes: Syria launched an official e-visa platform (evisa.sy) in May 2024, coordinated by the Ministries of Tourism, Interior, Foreign Affairs, and Communications. As of mid-2026 the platform displays 'EVisa Coming Soon' and is not operational. The evisa.sy domain has SSL certificate errors preventing direct content verification.

visa_policy.notes: Syria's visa policy changed significantly after the fall of the Assad regime in December 2024. The transitional government opened the country to visa-on-arrival for nearly all nationalities from January 2025. Official Syrian government websites (mofaex.gov.sy, mots.gov.sy, evisa.sy) do not publish an enumerated visa exemption or visa-on-arrival nationality list accessible in English. Detailed fee tiers in this record are sourced from secondary sources referencing official Decree No. 148/8 of July 6 2025. Israeli nationals are permanently barred. Iranian nationals require advance authorization and are not eligible for VOA. Passports with Israeli stamps result in denial of entry. Air travelers at Damascus and Aleppo airports generally require prior entry approval (OK to board) obtained before departure. Most sources agree on visa-free status for: Algeria, Jordan, Lebanon (with caveats), Mauritania, Morocco, Sudan, Tunisia, Turkey, Yemen. Serbia and Malaysia appear fee-exempt but may require a formal VOA stamp. GCC nationals are eligible for VOA. Pre-approval (3-4 weeks processing) is required for various nationalities including India, China, Pakistan, Afghanistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Vietnam, Nepal, Egypt, Yemen, Sudan, Libya, and others — but sources conflict on the exact list.

conditional_access.conditions: Persons with Syrian parentage (Syrian father, and possibly Syrian mother or spouse) are exempt from visa fees regardless of their foreign nationality. This includes dual nationals. Entry rules otherwise follow the traveler's foreign nationality.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program exists in Syria. No official Syrian government source references any such program.

validation_notes: ADDED to visa_free: Algeria (DZA), Morocco (MAR), Sudan (SDN), Tunisia (TUN), Turkey (TUR), Yemen (YEM) — these were missing from the prior record but are consistently listed as visa-free by Wikipedia's Visa policy of Syria article and corroborated by inyourpocket.com and syriascopetravel.com.

MOVED Malaysia (MYS) from visa_free to visa_on_arrival: Wikipedia lists Malaysia as a 15-day VOA recipient (not true visa-free), though fee-exempt. Multiple sources conflict; recorded as fee-exempt VOA with 15-day stay.
MOVED Serbia (SRB) from visa_free to visa_on_arrival: Serbian MFA explicitly states Serbian citizens require a visa for Syria; fee-exempt VOA is the accurate classification.

ADDED Iran restriction to visa_on_arrival notes and general notes: Iran requires advance authorization from a Syrian embassy; Iranians are NOT eligible for VOA — multiple sources confirm this. The prior record noted Iran required 'pre-approval' ambiguously within the broad VOA entry but did not distinguish it clearly.
ADDED GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) bloc as a VOA entry: Wikipedia and inyourpocket.com list GCC nationals as VOA-eligible — this was absent from the prior record.

CORRECTED Lebanon classification: Lebanon was in visa_free with a 180-day max stay but the 180-day figure is unverified and multiple sources indicate Lebanese without foreign residence/Syrian family need advance visas. Reclassified as visa_free with caveats and null max stay.
ADDED conditional_access entry for Syrian-origin diaspora (fee exemption for those with Syrian parentage) — this was missing from the prior record.

CORRECTED visa_on_arrival broad entry: removed claim that fee tiers included 'Gabon/Kyrgyzstan $200' and 'Marshall Islands/Congo/North Korea $300' as these are inconsistent with 2026 secondary sources. Updated fee notes based on more recent syrianguides.com and syriascopetravel.com data.
RETAINED e-visa as non-operational ('Coming Soon') — confirmed by multiple 2026 sources.
All source_official flags for Wikipedia and travel guide entries set to false as these are not official Syrian government domains.
gaps: Official Syrian government websites (mofaex.gov.sy, mots.gov.sy, evisa.sy) do not publish an enumerated visa-free or visa-on-arrival nationality list in English — all classification data relies on secondary sources or Wikipedia.
The evisa.sy e-visa platform remains non-operational as of mid-2026 ('Coming Soon'); SSL certificate errors prevent direct content verification.
Maximum stay days for the vast majority of VOA and visa-free categories not confirmed from official Syrian government sources.
Exact pre-approval nationality list is inconsistent across secondary sources (India, China, Pakistan, Afghanistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Vietnam, Nepal are commonly cited, but lists vary).
The actual text of Decree No. 148/8 (July 6 2025) was not accessible on any official Syrian government domain.
GCC nationals' maximum stay duration for VOA not confirmed.
Algeria, Morocco, Sudan, Tunisia, Turkey, Yemen maximum stay for visa-free entry not confirmed.

Lebanon's precise entry rule (fee-free VOA vs true visa-free, and conditions for those without foreign residency or Syrian family) is ambiguous; the $60 flat fee noted in syrianguides.com fee table conflicts with visa-free classification in Wikipedia.
Iran's exact entry prohibition vs. advance-visa-required distinction not confirmed from official Syrian government domain.
Status of pre-2025 Arab League bilateral free-movement agreements under the current transitional government not confirmed from official Syrian sources.
Conditional entry rules based on held third-country credentials (US/Schengen/UK visa or residence permit) could not be confirmed from any official Syrian government domain.
No official Syrian government source documents bilateral diplomatic/service/official passport visa waiver agreements under the transitional government.
Syria was reinstated in the Arab League in 2023; however, no specific bilateral or multilateral free-movement treaty text was confirmed from official Syrian government domains.
Airport pre-approval (OK to board) process details and exact nationalities exempt from it are not published on official Syrian government websites.

outbound_official.note: no official Syria government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data
